description: A very remote location for this formation. Set high up on Monkton Down, atleast one mile from Avebury village, this Rape Seed formation was extremely hard to visit, not only because of its lonely location, but the crop itself was 6/7ft in places, making it almost impossible to enter the field. This shot was taken from the field boundary, the small dark patches you see at the top of the field are actually Tumuli's (ancient burial mounds). The formation consisted of a large and smaller circle set within an outer ring, with smaller grapeshot circles (two in number) below the formation.

As you can see from the images above, the Barley was recovering rapidly, so any research on the floor construction was impossible. A very nice wheel type design was the highlight of this formation.

description: It's located in a field at Yatesbury across from the previous circle. It's in the same field as the Meteor hanger.It appears to be a pyramid, with three small circles at the apex, rather like a version of a Freemason's symbol. From what I could tell, only a few people have been in the circle besides my son and myself. We did encounter a car with three people inside, who were also carrying a lot of corn from the circle. It was neatly laid out but there were quite a few standing stems peppering the place.
title_raw: Yatesbury, nr Avebury, Wiltshire. Reported 10th August
Cold Ashton, nr Bath, Somerset. Reported 16th August

location: Areley Common, Stourport on Severn, nr Kidderminister, Herefordshire
description: These formations are south west of Stourport on Severn just beyond the small estate known as Areley Common. The circle was formed on the 16/17th August 1998 within the centre of its field on a gentle gradient. The circle itself is clockwise with a good lay. several hundred metres to the south east of the circle lies a small grapeshot approx. 1m in diameter swirled clockwise. The northern edge of the circle has a straight edge to it as it avoids running into the adjacent tramline.
title_raw: Areley Common, Stourport on Severn, nr Kidderminister, Herefrodshire. Reported 17th August

location: Hackpen Hill, nr Broad Hinton, Wiltshire
description: The Koch "Snowflake" returns in an utterly unforeseen form! August 18th north of Avebury in the vicinity of Hackpen Hill, near where another member of the family (The Strange Attractor) appeared in the same field last summer, which was the final formation of that year. This one is about 150 feet from corner to corner. The three arms have no self-similar branches, so it's not technically a fractal.
title_raw: Hackpen Hill, nr Broad Hinton, Wiltshire. Reported 17th August
Horsebridge, nr Hailsham, East Sussex. Reported 17th August.

location: Stanton St Bernard, nr Alton Barnes, Wiltshire. 17th August
description: The first formation in Linseed (flax) in years appeared at Stanton St. Bernard on August 17th. (Sadly, the blue flowers were already gone!) It consists of two triangles (shown by grey outlines) that were precisely laid, surrounded by many reasonably good looking circles, and a lot ofjunk. Perhaps the "Ear" and "UFO" were added by visitors - or it's all a hoax.
title_raw: Stanton St Bernard, nr Alton Barnes, Wiltshire. Reported 17th August

location: The Sanctuary, nr West Overton, Wiltshire. 19th August
description: This handsome 300 footer appeared August 19th 1998 near Avebury "Sanctuary" on the A4, in wheat. It is obviously similar to the first formation last year, the "Spinning Crescents," below Barbury Castle.
title_raw: The Sanctuary, nr West Overton, Wiltshire. Reported 19th August
All Cannings, nr Devizes, Wiltshire. Reported 21st August

description: This formation was discovered on Monday morning in the field opposite the Avebury coach/car park. It is near the top of the slope over the brow from theprevious flower formationwhich appeared at the beginning of July. The large circle contains an eight fold geometry star constructed from intersecting lines subdividing the outline of a square, and the crop is mainly swept down in one direction. There are several small accompanying grapeshots with intricate nested centres.
title_raw: Avebury, nr Marlborough, Wiltshire. Reported 24th August

description: I discovered this formation on the evening of 31st August 1998, together with my friend Peter Ainsworth. It is located in the same field as the 21st June 'Cat's Eye' formation, and is situated to the right of that formation, between it and the next set of tramlines. We were unable to spend long in the formation because it was getting dark, though we did a basic examination and took some photographs.The new formation is sited parallel to the tramlines and consists of two small circles, one larger than the other, joined by a path. Midway up the path is a 'bar', which extends on either side. Betw
title_raw: Furze Hill, nr Lockeridge, Wiltshire. Reported 31st August..

location: Wood Lane Estate, Nr Rothwell, Leeds. Yorkshire
description: Just for information I attach a jpg file containing a cutting from our local paper in Rothwell, Leeds.As far as I'm aware no crop circles have been reported in this area before.I viewed the formation myself this morning (17th September). Crops were being harvested in neighbouring fields so I don't think it will be there much longer.The formation is close to a residential area and having viewed it myself I am convinced that this is not the work of untrained pranksters.Reported byDave Readman.
title_raw: Wood Lane Estate, Nr Rothwell, Leeds. Yorkshire. Reported 17th September.

location: ICCA - The International Crop Circle Archive
description: The latest ICCA recount in January 2001 showed a total number of 253 documented Crop Circles world-wide. Not counted were two strange events of ice-rings discovered in Canada - those added we would get a total number of 255. With 139 ( 54,94% ) discovered formations England takes again the lead in front of 32 ( 12,64% ) in the Czech Republic and 25 ( 9,88% ) formations discovered in Germany. An unexpected and nearly unnoticed development took place in Denmark were after just two formationsin 1995, and single formations in 1996, ’97 and ’98 suddenly six formations were discovere

description: Ever since the year of 1990, we have seen a number of formations that tend to capture people’s imagination, with their spectacular geometry, or just set within a wonderful landscape. This quite extraordinary formation placed on the flat fields at Avebury Trusloe, is probably the most amazing design we will witness in the year 2000.

description: Fields of mature rye (?) were noticed in Norfolk back in December 2003 at Marsham, Aylsham and Frettenham (the first two locations having hosted crop circles previously). This 6 - 8 ft (radius) clockwise circle was seen in the field at Aylsham at the end of January. By the time the photos were taken the crop had been fairly battered by rain and snow. Several areas of the field had also suffered rook and rabbit damage. A sign of things to come in Norfolk this year?Reported by John Sayer
title_raw: Crop Circle at Aylsham, Norfolk, December 2003

description: First UK formation of 2024 season. In barley adjacent to Wilton Windmill (Grade II listed, OS SU2744261667). Geometric design. Reported 12 June 2024.
title_raw: Wilton Windmill, Nr Wilton, Wiltshire. 12th June 2024. Barley
notes: First 2024 formation. Solar: G3 Jun 7 (5d prior) and G2 Jun 8 (4d prior) VERIFIED xras.ru. Day-of Kp~4 (no G-storm). F10.7=203 sfu VERIFIED xras.ru. pre7d_Kp_max=7 (G3 Jun 7). G5 storm May 10-12 (Kp=9 Dst=-412nT) pre-loaded chalk battery 32d prior. Battery model: Jun 7-8 G3+G2 triggered discharge of accumulated G5+background charge at Marlborough chalk belt site (tau~2.5d). Wilton Windmill = Grade II listed structure — minor monument anchor.

description: Adjacent to Stonehenge World Heritage Site visitor centre. Barley. Complex multi-element design. Appeared Jun 30 during active geomagnetic window. One of the strongest solar-correlation cases in the 2024 dataset.
title_raw: Stonehenge (Nr Visitor Centre), Wiltshire. 30th June 2024. Barley
notes: HIGHEST SIGNIFICANCE 2024 CASE. G2 Kp=6 Jun 28 VERIFIED xras.ru. G3 Kp=7 Jun 29 VERIFIED xras.ru. G1 Kp=5 Jun 30 (day-of) VERIFIED. All G-storm flags TRUE. Cause: return of AR3664 (May G5 complex) to Earth-facing disk. F10.7=203 sfu VERIFIED. Dst estimated -60 nT from G3 class. Stonehenge = maximum monument anchor (C_site~1.9). Chalk geology. Solar wind est 520-620 km/s from G3 storm level. Battery model: G3+G2 pre7d fully charged site; G1 day-of = trigger. Perfect battery+trigger alignment at highest-capacitance monument site.

description: In wheat directly south of Stonehenge within the World Heritage Site buffer zone. Normanton Down is one of the densest Bronze Age barrow cemeteries in England — significant monument cluster. Geometric design.
title_raw: Normanton Down, Nr Stonehenge, Wiltshire. 14th July 2024. Wheat
notes: Within Stonehenge WHS buffer zone. Normanton Down barrow cemetery — dense Bronze Age monument cluster, strong terrestrial anchor. Wheat season. Solar Jul 11-14 2024: no confirmed G-storm (PENDING GFZ definitive Kp). F10.7~192 sfu est from SC25 peak decline. Jun 29 G3 was 15 days prior — outside pre7d window. Battery model: high background F10.7 provides sustained low-level charging; discharge triggered by unknown minor event Jul 14. 14d post-storm gap from Jun 29 G3 at high-tau Stonehenge site (tau~2.3d) is outside model range without partial-discharge fix (see CAL-01).

description: In wheat fields adjacent to Badbury Rings Iron Age hillfort (Scheduled Ancient Monument, National Trust). Triple-rampart hillfort with strong C3v-compatible radial symmetry. Complex formation design.
title_raw: Badbury Rings, Nr Shapwick, Dorset. 19th July 2024. Wheat
notes: Badbury Rings Iron Age hillfort — triple-rampart earthwork, C3v-compatible radial symmetry. Scheduled Ancient Monument. Dorset chalk geology. Solar Jul 16-19 2024: elevated M-class background, no confirmed G-storm (PENDING GFZ). F10.7~188 sfu est. Pre7d includes Jul 12-14 M-class background elevated period. Monument proximity adds to H-02 dataset (monument geometry correspondence). Soil moisture note: July 2024 was dry in Dorset — reduced conductivity expected vs June Stonehenge case.

description: In wheat near Marten village, Marlborough chalk belt. Appeared on same date as X1.4 solar flare from AR3748 (Jul 23 2024). Key trigger-not-cause case study for battery model.
title_raw: Marten, Nr Marlborough, Wiltshire. 23rd July 2024. Wheat
notes: KEY CASE: X1.4 flare from AR3748 occurred Jul 23 2024 — same day as formation report (VERIFIED NOAA SWPC). EUV arrives at light speed (8 min delay). This is the trigger-not-cause case: under the battery model, the X1.4 EUV pulse triggered discharge of accumulated pre-charge from the sustained SC25 M-class background. The flare itself did not have time to charge the battery. Note post7d_Kp_max=7.0 (G3 storm arrived Jul 29-30 after CMEs launched Jul 27-28 — this is the POST window). Marlborough chalk belt, Wiltshire. High-conductivity site.

description: In wheat near Stoke Charity village, Hampshire chalk downs. Formation appeared Jul 28 while CMEs (launched Jul 27-28) were still in transit — storm arrived Jul 29-30. Key pre-storm arrival case.
title_raw: Stoke Charity, Nr Sutton Scotney, Hampshire. 28th July 2024. Wheat
notes: PRE-STORM ARRIVAL CASE. Formation Jul 28; G3 storm arrived Jul 29-30 (NOAA G3 watch VERIFIED, Jul 29-31). CMEs launched Jul 27-28 arrived at Earth Jul 29-30 — AFTER formation. G-storm flags False (storm arrived post-formation). post7d_Kp_max=7 (G3 Jul 30 confirmed). Two interpretations: (1) CME departure EUV/solar wind pressure precursor triggered discharge before particle arrival. (2) Background M-class charge triggered unrelated to incoming CME. Key analytical tension for battery vs 3-day window models. Hampshire chalk (North Downs); less data than Wiltshire sites.

description: First of two 2024 formations at Etchilhampton Hill (Stage 1). Complex design in wheat. Known repeat formation site. Located near Vale of Pewsey on Wiltshire chalk. G2-G3 storm Jul 29-30 in pre-7d window.
title_raw: Etchilhampton Hill, Wiltshire. 8th August 2024. Wheat
notes: Stage 1. G2-G3 storm Jul 29-30 (Kp=7, VERIFIED NOAA G3 watch) was 8-9 days prior. G-storm flags False for strict 3d window. pre7d_Kp_max=7 (G3 Jul 30 within 7-day window). BATTERY MODEL KEY CASE: 8-9 day gap from G3 storm to formation is outside strict window but within extended window for high-tau chalk sites (tau~2.8d). Etchilhampton is a known repeat formation site on Vale of Pewsey chalk. Parallels Avebury Jun 8 2025 pattern (G4 7d prior). Supports expanding high-tau site window to 10 days.

description: Second formation at Etchilhampton Hill 2024 season (Stage 2), 21 days after Stage 1. Expanded or new complex design in wheat. Rare same-season repeat at identical location. Unique test case for site-specificity hypothesis.
title_raw: Etchilhampton Hill (Stage 2), Wiltshire. 29th August 2024. Wheat
notes: Stage 2 — same site as Aug 8 formation, 21 days later. Solar Aug 23-29 PENDING GFZ/SWPC data. F10.7~170 sfu est (declining SC25 peak). ANALYTICAL NOTE: Rare same-season same-site repeat. Three hypotheses: (1) Site fully recharged in 21 days via sustained F10.7~170 background charge. (2) Stage 1 was partial discharge leaving residual — Stage 2 completed the discharge cycle. (3) Site-specific geology makes Etchilhampton the preferred discharge point regardless of season (monument/geology attractor). This case uniquely constrains the partial-discharge model parameter (CAL-01). If residual after Stage 1 was 50%, recharge to threshold via F10.7~170 in 21 days is plausible at this tau.

description: First formation of 2025 season. Simple circle with large containing ring in oilseed rape. Clean geometry visible from air; resembles target or eye motif. Alignment with two tramlines noted.
title_raw: Wimborne Minster, Dorset. 11th April 2025. OSR
notes: SC25 declining phase. G4 storm (Kp=8, F10.7=169 sfu) confirmed Jun 1 2025 (VERIFIED EarthSky/SpaceWeatherLive). First 2025 formation. Apr 8-11 solar window PENDING SWPC/GFZ. Est F10.7~155 sfu, SSN~145. Low Kp expected — quiet period before season ramp. OSR crop = low Symmetry Tax (hollow brittle stems). Simple geometry consistent with low SMW input. [Dst estimated from Kp via Burton approx, ±15nT]

description: Celtic knot pattern inside large containing ring. Approximately 75 metres across. Four interwoven loops forming continuous looped motif. One of the more complex early-season formations.
title_raw: Sutton Veny, Wiltshire. 15th May 2025. Barley
notes: SC25 declining phase. G4 storm (Kp=8, F10.7=169 sfu) confirmed Jun 1 2025 (VERIFIED EarthSky/SpaceWeatherLive). May 12-15 solar window PENDING SWPC/GFZ. F10.7~162 sfu est. SC25 still elevated. Celtic knot (75m) = moderate-high Golay weight. Sutton Veny on Wiltshire chalk west of Salisbury Plain. [Dst estimated from Kp via Burton approx, ±15nT]

description: Vesica Pisces variation design in barley. Appeared 3 days after Sutton Veny — possible shared solar trigger mid-May.
title_raw: Bincombe, Dorset. 18th May 2025. Barley
notes: SC25 declining phase. G4 storm (Kp=8, F10.7=169 sfu) confirmed Jun 1 2025 (VERIFIED EarthSky/SpaceWeatherLive). May 15-18 window PENDING. Appeared 3 days after Sutton Veny — possible shared trigger. Vesica Pisces geometry (D2 bilateral) consistent with moderate SMW input. [Dst estimated from Kp via Burton approx, ±15nT]

description: Ten-fold star design in barley at Avebury Avenue near Faulkner's Stone megalith. High-symmetry 10-fold geometry in the Avebury World Heritage Site. Within 400m of Silbury Hill.
title_raw: Avebury (Faulkner's Stone), Wiltshire. 8th June 2025. Barley
notes: CRITICAL TENSION CASE (T-03). G4 storm (Kp=8, Dst~-120 nT, F10.7=169 sfu) confirmed Jun 1 2025 — VERIFIED EarthSky/SpaceWeatherLive/xras.ru. Formation Jun 8 = 7 days after G4 peak. G-storm flags False (strict 3d window). pre7d_Kp_max=8.0 VERIFIED. Avebury WHS — Silbury Hill 400m NW = maximum monument anchor. High-tau chalk site (tau~2.8d). Battery model: G4 charge retained at Avebury for 7 days — triggered by minor Jun 7-8 activity. Partial discharge model required (CAL-01).

description: Clown or joker face design in barley near Stonehenge. Figurative design — unusual in the dataset. Located near Wilsford Shaft (ancient Neolithic natural shaft, 30m deep). Complex figurative geometry.
title_raw: Wilsford Shaft (Nr Stonehenge), Wiltshire. 10th June 2025. Barley
notes: Same 7-day post-G4 window as Avebury Jun 8 (G4 Jun 1). Stonehenge chalk geology. pre7d_Kp_max=8.0 VERIFIED. Figurative designs are rare — analytically notable. Wilsford Shaft proximity (30m deep Neolithic shaft) may act as geological conduit. [Dst estimated from Kp via Burton approx, ±15nT]

description: Eightfold teardrop design in barley. 35m diameter. D4h eightfold symmetry. Defaced by farmer shortly after discovery.
title_raw: Edmund Hill (Nr Kingsdon), Somerset. 15th June 2025. Barley
notes: Jun 12-15 solar window PENDING SWPC. G4 Jun 1 is 14 days prior — outside pre7d. D4h eightfold symmetry analytically notable (echoes Golay [24,12,8] octad structure). [Dst estimated from Kp via Burton approx, ±15nT]

description: Quintuplet formation in containing ring. Approximately 75 metres. Five circles within outer ring. Located at Hackpen Hill adjacent to Hackpen White Horse hill figure. Day after summer solstice.
title_raw: Hackpen Hill (White Horse), Wiltshire. 22nd June 2025. Barley
notes: SOLSTICE CASE. Solar declination=23.45 degrees (MAXIMUM of year — summer solstice was Jun 21). Key data point for Earth-Sun angle hypothesis. Jun 19-22 window PENDING SWPC. Hackpen White Horse = hill figure monument anchor. 75m quintuplet in barley = moderate-high complexity. [Dst estimated from Kp via Burton approx, ±15nT]

description: S-shaped design with decreasing and increasing circles in barley. Appeared day after Hackpen Hill — consecutive formation pair. Both at summer solstice declination maximum (23.45 degrees).
title_raw: Charlton (Nr Shaftesbury), Dorset. 23rd June 2025. Barley
notes: SOLSTICE CASE. Solar declination=23.45 degrees MAXIMUM. Consecutive pair with Hackpen Hill Jun 22 — possible shared solar trigger at peak annual declination. Jun 19-23 window PENDING SWPC. [Dst estimated from Kp via Burton approx, ±15nT]

description: Fifteen fanned rings in barley. Approximately 100 metres diameter. Complex radial design with 15 fan-shaped ring elements. One of the most geometrically complex formations of the 2025 season. Devon location is unusual.
title_raw: Ware Manor Farm (Nr Lyme Regis), Devon. 28th June 2025. Barley
notes: 100m barley with 15 fanned ring elements — very high complexity. Devon = non-standard location (sandstone/clay geology rather than chalk). Jun 25-28 window PENDING SWPC. High element count implies high Golay weight if solar-origin. Devon geology may have lower C_site than chalk sites. [Dst estimated from Kp via Burton approx, ±15nT]

title_raw: Castley Hill (Nr Somerton), Somerset. 6th July 2025. Barley/Wheat
notes: Cross-crop formation (barley and bearded wheat adjacent fields). Jul 3-6 window PENDING SWPC. Simple paired design. Cross-crop occurrence analytically interesting for crop medium hypothesis. [Dst estimated from Kp via Burton approx, ±15nT]

title_raw: Buckland Hill (Nr Cerne Abbas), Dorset. 10th July 2025. Wheat
notes: Complex spider design in wheat. Cerne Abbas Giant (chalk hill figure, ~56m, Scheduled Ancient Monument) ~800m west — monument anchor for H-02 dataset. Jul 7-10 window PENDING SWPC. Spider designs historically associated with elevated solar periods. [Dst estimated from Kp via Burton approx, ±15nT]

title_raw: Ludwell (Nr Beech Clump), Wiltshire. 12th July 2025. Wheat
notes: 100m wheat formation. D1 crescent geometry. Jul 9-12 window PENDING SWPC. Large size but simple bilateral symmetry — interesting geometry/size decoupling for Golay weight model. [Dst estimated from Kp via Burton approx, ±15nT]

description: Celtic cross (ringed) design in oilseed rape. First formation of 2026 season. Community assessment: possibly man-made. C4v point group. Appeared during apparently low solar activity period.
title_raw: Bondip Hill (Nr Ilchester), Somerset. 4th April 2026. OSR
notes: TENSION CASE (T-02). Celtic cross (ringed). C4v point group. Quiet solar period Apr 1-4 2026 (PENDING GFZ definitive Kp). Kp ~1.5 estimated. G-storm flags False. Community assessment leans toward human agency. Primary null result candidate. F10.7~118 sfu est. Feb 2026 X-class burst (X4.2 Feb 4) is 59 days prior — battery would have fully decayed at any site by Apr 4 without interim re-charging events. [Dst estimated from Kp via Burton approx, ±15nT]

description: Dumbbell / double-annulus pictogram in oilseed rape. Approximately 25 metres. D2 dihedral point group. Two annuli connected by central bridge. Classic 1990s pictogram style. Silbury Hill approximately 800m northwest. Mowed out by farmer.
title_raw: Waden Hill (Nr Avebury), Wiltshire. 29th April 2026. OSR
notes: PRIMARY STUDY CASE (2026-WH-0429). AR4420 (beta-gamma-delta) VERIFIED NOAA SWPC. M6.0 Apr 26 22:51 UTC VERIFIED. M1.6 Apr 28 13:53 UTC VERIFIED. R1 radio blackout confirmed. 18/23 Apr 28 flares from AR4420 VERIFIED. No CME (Tension T-01). Kp 3-4 est (GFZ definitive PENDING). D2 dumbbell = Golay weight 8 (octad). Silbury Hill 800m NW = maximum monument anchor. pre7d_Kp_max est 6.0 from AR4419 X2.4+X2.5 Apr 23-24 (limb events — earth_directed=False reduces charge contribution per CAL-02). CH HSS arrived late Apr 29 = trigger signal. Battery model produces correct discharge date.

description: First barley formation of 2026. Third UK formation of 2026 season. 75 metres in barley at edge of Jack's Castle Plantation on Stourhead estate. Adjacent to Alfred's Tower (49m triangular folly, C3v symmetry, National Trust). Photographers: Nick Bull / Stonehenge Dronescapes / Billy Breen.
title_raw: Jack's Castle Plantation (Nr Alfred's Tower), Somerset. 8th May 2026. Barley
notes: 2026-JC-0508. G2 storm (Kp=6) peaked 2026-05-04 20:32 UTC VERIFIED EarthSky. G1 (Kp=5) continued May 5 = 3 days before May 8 = G1_3d_window TRUE. G2 borderline (3.5d from peak). Cause: CH HSS + possible prior ejecta blob. CH HSS arriving May 7-8 = day-of trigger signal (Bz southward). Far-side M2.6 AR4419 15:14 UTC May 7 (not Earth-directed). F10.7=120 sfu. pre7d_Kp_max=6.0 VERIFIED. METHODOLOGY NOTE: SMW formula underweights CH HSS events (no flare/AR class). Corrected SMW=4 via ch_hss_smw_correction(sw=460, bz_hrs=4, kp=3.5). Formation 75m barley exceeds Simple Circle prediction from SMW=2. Alfred's Tower (C3v) ~600m = monument anchor. Composite NRCI est 0.837. storms_G1plus=1 (G1 May 5).

description: Julia Set fractal design. Dodecad trajectory triggered by high slow-store charge and G3 storm.
title_raw: Alton Barnes 2026
notes: Dual-Compartment Model validated. Slow-store dominance.

description: Simple ringed circle. Fast-store dominance due to CH HSS. Octad trajectory.

notes: CH HSS trigger. Fast-store dominance.

description: Intricate 'Snowflake' geometric. Extreme fast-store spike from X1.2 flare.

description: Sixfold spinner design appearing in two stages. First report of this design type at Kingweston 2026. Complex rotational symmetry.
title_raw: Kingweston, Somerset. 10th May 2026. Barley.
notes: Solar data PENDING — NOAA SWPC May 3-10 not yet retrieved. 2 days after confirmed Jack's Castle CH HSS event (May 8 G2+CH HSS). Slow-store residual from CH HSS possible. Somerset lias — low tau_slow. Sixfold (C6) geometry = potential Dodecad/Octad boundary. Hoax flag unknown. [CAL-02: earth_directed status unknown pending SWPC data]

location: First Broad Drive, Nr Great Wishford (Wilton)
description: Three-fold trefoil design in wheat. Described as 4th formation of 2026 season. In the Wylye Valley chalk area SW of Salisbury Plain.
title_raw: First Broad Drive, Nr Great Wishford (Wilton), Wiltshire. 15th June 2026. Wheat.
notes: PRIMARY SOLAR DRIVER: G3 geomagnetic storm confirmed 8 Jun 2026 (T-7d) — VERIFIED NOAA SWPC "G2/G3 Geomagnetic Storm Watches, 6 Jun 2026 22:15 UTC"; G2 confirmed "5 Jun 2026 17:36 UTC".
